positional_bias.note: Max share 0.333 is below the 0.40 threshold. All four positions used.

length_bias.sidecar_desync_note: Sidecar reports length_bias_violations=0 but two questions (q1 ratio=0.226, q5 ratio=0.240) exceed the per-question 0.20 flag threshold. The aggregate rate (0.333) does not breach the 0.40 fail threshold so the check passes, but the sidecar field is inaccurate.

weakest_questions.note: Two distractors in q4 invoke rationales (creativity, regulatory compliance) that are not closely connected to common beginner misconceptions about simplest-tier-first; they are wrong but not absurd.

los_from_corpus: LO1 — Name and describe the four tiers of AI system complexity: direct call, chained calls, RAG, and agent
LO2 — State the defining criterion for each tier — what makes a task belong to that tier and not a simpler one
LO3 — Apply a four-question decision matrix to a new task description and identify the correct tier
LO4 — Explain why starting with the simplest tier that works is the right engineering default
LO5 — Recognize the two most common design mistakes: over-engineering (reaching for an agent when a direct call suffices) and under-engineering (skipping RAG when the model is likely to hallucinate)

beginner_fairness_and_scope.notes: LLM and ReAct are prerequisite vocabulary from prior topics (14.5, 14.6) — explicitly listed in corpus §2. All stems and distractors are within the topic boundary.

soft_findings: LENGTH_BIAS_BORDERLINE: q1 correct answer is 22.6% longer than mean distractors (threshold 20%). q5 correct answer is 24.0% longer. Aggregate rate 0.333 passes the 0.40 threshold, but builder should tighten distractor lengths in q1 and q5.

SIDECAR_FIELD_INACCURATE: quiz.meta.json reports length_bias_violations=0; two questions (q1, q5) exceed the per-question 0.20 flag threshold. Sidecar should be corrected for accurate downstream reporting.

DISTRACTOR_QUALITY_Q4: D1 (creativity rationale) and D2 (regulatory prohibition) in q4 are wrong but only weakly connected to plausible beginner misconceptions about the simplest-tier-first rule. Consider replacing with misconceptions closer to the corpus rationale (e.g., 'simpler tiers are less powerful so engineers avoid them' or 'agents have more built-in error recovery').
verdict: Pass — positional bias 0.333, length bias 0.333, distractor plausibility 0.0 absurd rate, all 5 LOs covered, no out-of-scope questions; two soft length-bias borderline questions (q1, q5) and one sidecar field inaccuracy noted but do not breach thresholds.
